prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Warhammer Fantasy / The Old World inspired grimdark fantasy map, diverse archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of a vast dark fantasy ocean filled with many island groups and microcontinents.
Subject: diverse archipelago layout with uneven island distribution across a broad ocean: several large islands, microcontinents, tiny islets, curved island arcs, lagoons, reef belts, narrow straits, remote atolls, volcanic islands, fragmented coasts, and irregular natural spacing. Include old empire island towns, cursed sea forts, dwarf mountain islands, blighted marsh islands, northern frozen islands, chaos-storm volcanic chain, dark forest islands, river deltas, and fortified sea routes.
Style/medium: dark fantasy map, old world cartography, sinister wilderness regions marked with non-text symbols, stained aged parchment, highly detailed hand-painted RPG sourcebook atlas.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, no single dominant mainland, island groups sweep in organic arcs and clusters, full archipelago visible with ocean border.
Lighting/mood: grimdark low fantasy, oppressive threatening sky, corruption spreading from the north, dangerous seas, crumbling empire outposts, desperate survival.
Color palette: smoky parchment, cold teal and blackened ocean, charcoal ink, muted greens, rust reds, sickly marsh tones, icy northern blues, volcanic red glows.
Materials/textures: stained parchment grain, engraved ink lines, painted terrain, detailed waves, shoals, reefs, soot and weathering.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid grid-like spacing and avoid identical island shapes.
